records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
The term "المأتم" (ma'tam) originally referred to a gathering of men and women for either sorrowful or joyful occasions. Over time, it became specifically associated with gatherings of women during times of mourning for the deceased. It has also been said to apply particularly to young women in mourning (Al-Nihaya).
Additionally, the term "المأتم" can also refer to food that is prepared for the deceased ("Fi").
records.arabicText: المأتم في الأصل: مجتمع الرجال والنساء في الغم والفرح، ثم خص باجتماع النساء للموت وقيل هو للشواب من النساء لا غير (النهاية) ويطلق على الطعام للميت (في)

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.502 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever consoles a grieving person will be clothed in a robe of honor on the Day of Standing (the Day of Judgment), which will bring them joy (and honor)."
records.arabicText: 502 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «مَنْ عَزَّى حَزِيناً كُسِيَ فِي اَلْمَوْقِفِ حُلَّةً يُحَبَّرُ بِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.502 -
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever consoles a grieving person will be clothed in a robe of honor on the Day of Standing (the Day of Judgment), which will bring them joy (and honor)."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.503 - It was narrated from Hisham ibn al-Hakam that He said:
"I saw Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) offering condolences both before and after the burial."
records.arabicText: 503 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ اَلْحَكَمِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ يُعَزِّي قَبْلَ اَلدَّفْنِ وَ بَعْدَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.503 - It was narrated from Hisham ibn al-Hakam that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) offering condolences both before and after the burial."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.504 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The obligatory act of offering condolences is after the burial."
records.arabicText: 504 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلتَّعْزِيَةُ اَلْوَاجِبَةُ بَعْدَ اَلدَّفْنِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.504 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The obligatory act of offering condolences is after the burial."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.505 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"It is sufficient as a form of condolence for the bereaved to see you."
records.arabicText: 505 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَفَاكَ مِنَ اَلتَّعْزِيَةِ بِأَنْ يَرَاكَ صَاحِبُ اَلْمُصِيبَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.505 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is sufficient as a form of condolence for the bereaved to see you."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.506 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) visited a group of people who had experienced a loss and said:
"May Allah (swt) strengthen you in your weakness, grant you beautiful patience, and have mercy on your deceased."
Then he departed.
records.arabicText: 506 - وَ أَتَى أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَوْماً قَدْ أُصِيبُوا بِمُصِيبَةٍ فَقَالَ «جَبَرَ اَللَّهُ وَهْنَكُمْ وَ أَحْسَنَ عَزَاكُمْ وَ رَحِمَ مُتَوَفَّاكُمْ» ثُمَّ اِنْصَرَفَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.506 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) visited a group of people who had experienced a loss and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"May Allah (swt) strengthen you in your weakness, grant you beautiful patience, and have mercy on your deceased."
Then he departed.

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.507 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Offering condolences leads to Paradise."
records.arabicText: 507 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلتَّعْزِيَةُ تُورِثُ اَلْجَنَّةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.507 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Offering condolences leads to Paradise."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.508 - The truthful one, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), offered condolences to a man who had lost his son and said to him:
"Allah (swt) is better for your son than you are, and the reward from Allah (swt) is better for you than your son."
Later, when the Imam heard of the man's continued grief, he returned to him and said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) also passed away; do you not take him as an example?"
The man responded that his son was on the verge of maturity (approaching adulthood).
The Imam then said (consoling him): "There are three things ahead of him: the testimony that there is no god but Allah (swt), Allah’s (swt) mercy, and the intercession of the Messenger of Allah (sw). He will not miss out on any of these, if Allah (swt) the Almighty wills."
records.arabicText: 508 - وَ عَزَّى اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلاً بِابْنٍ لَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «اَللَّهُ خَيْرٌ لاِبْنِكَ مِنْكَ وَ ثَوَابُ اَللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لَكَ مِنْهُ» فَبَلَغَهُ جَزَعُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَعَادَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ «قَدْ مَاتَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَ فَمَا لَكَ بِهِ أُسْوَةٌ» فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ مُرَاهِقاً فَقَالَ لَهُ «إِنَّ أَمَامَهُ ثَلاَثَ خِصَالٍ شَهَادَةَ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ وَ رَحْمَةَ اَللَّهِ وَ شَفَاعَةَ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَلَنْ تَفُوتَهُ وَاحِدَةٌ مِنْهُنَّ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.508 - The truthful one, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), offered condolences to a man who had lost his son and said to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Allah (swt) is better for your son than you are, and the reward from Allah (swt) is better for you than your son."
Later, when the Imam heard of the man's continued grief, he returned to him and said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) also passed away; do you not take him as an example?"
The man responded that his son was on the verge of maturity (approaching adulthood).
The Imam then said (consoling him): "There are three things ahead of him: the testimony that there is no god but Allah (swt), Allah’s (swt) mercy, and the intercession of the Messenger of Allah (sw). He will not miss out on any of these, if Allah (swt) the Almighty wills."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.509 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"It is proper for the person responsible for the funeral (i.e., the chief mourner) not to wear an outer cloak (ridā') and to remain in a shirt (Qamis) so that they are recognized (as grieving). It is also proper for the neighbors to provide food on their behalf for three days.”
records.arabicText: 509 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «يَنْبَغِي لِصَاحِبِ اَلْجِنَازَةِ أَنْ لاَ يَلْبَسَ رِدَاءً وَ أَنْ يَكُونَ فِي قَمِيصٍ حَتَّى يُعْرَفَ وَ يَنْبَغِي لِجِيرَانِهِ أَنْ يُطْعِمُوا عَنْهُ ثَلاَثَةَ أَيَّامٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.509 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is proper for the person responsible for the funeral (i.e., the chief mourner) not to wear an outer cloak (ridā') and to remain in a shirt (Qamis) so that they are recognized (as grieving). It is also proper for the neighbors to provide food on their behalf for three days.”

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.510 - Imam (as) said: "Cursed!, cursed is the one who puts down (removes) their cloak (ridā') in the misfortune of someone else."
records.arabicText: 510 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ وَضَعَ رِدَاءَهُ فِي مُصِيبَةِ غَيْرِهِ.

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.511 - "When Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) passed away, Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as), was seen coming out of the house with his shirt torn from the front and the back."
records.arabicText: 511 - وَ لَمَّا قُبِضَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْعَسْكَرِيُّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رُئِيَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَدْ خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلدَّارِ وَ قَدْ شُقَّ قَمِيصُهُ مِنْ خَلْفٍ وَ قُدَّامٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.511 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as) passed away, Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as), was seen coming out of the house with his shirt torn from the front and the back."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.512 - "The Messenger of Allah (sw) placed his cloak down during the funeral of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh (may Allah (swt) have mercy on him).
When asked about this, He (sw) said:
"I saw the angels had placed their cloaks down, so I placed mine as well."
records.arabicText: 512 - وَ قَدْ وَضَعَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ رِدَاءَهُ فِي جِنَازَةِ سَعْدِ بْنِ مُعَاذٍ رَحِمَهُ اَللَّهُ فَسُئِلَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ «إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةَ قَدْ وَضَعَتْ أَرْدِيَتَهَا فَوَضَعْتُ رِدَائِي.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.512 - "The Messenger of Allah (sw) placed his cloak down during the funeral of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh (may Allah (swt) have mercy on him).
When asked about this, He (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw the angels had placed their cloaks down, so I placed mine as well."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.513 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If patience had not been created before trials, the believer would have shattered like an egg striking a rock."
records.arabicText: 513 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لَوْ لاَ أَنَّ اَلصَّبْرَ خُلِقَ قَبْلَ اَلْبَلاَءِ لَتَفَطَّرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ كَمَا تَتَفَطَّرُ اَلْبَيْضَةُ عَلَى اَلصَّفَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.513 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If patience had not been created before trials, the believer would have shattered like an egg striking a rock."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.514 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"There are four qualities; whoever possesses them will be in the greatest light of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious:"
One whose protection is in the testimony that there is no god but Allah (swt) and that I am the Messenger of Allah (sw),
And who, when afflicted with a calamity, says:
Innā lillāhi wa innā ilayhi rāji‘ūn
"Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return"
And who, when blessed with goodness, says:
Al-ḥamdu lillāhi Rabbi al-‘ālamīn
"All praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds"
And one who, when committing a sin, says:
Astaghfiru Allāha wa atūbu ilayh
"I seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and repent to Him."
records.arabicText: 514 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «أَرْبَعٌ مَنْ كُنَّ فِيهِ كَانَ فِي نُورِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اَلْأَعْظَمِ مَنْ كَانَ عِصْمَةُ أَمْرِهِ شَهَادَةَ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ وَ أَنِّي رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُ مُصِيبَةٌ قَالَ «إِنّٰا لِلّٰهِ وَ إِنّٰا إِلَيْهِ رٰاجِعُونَ» وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَ خَيْراً قَالَ «اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ اَلْعٰالَمِينَ» وَ مَنْ إِذَا أَصَابَ خَطِيئَةً قَالَ «أَسْتَغْفِرُ اَللَّهَ وَ أَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.514 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There are four qualities; whoever possesses them will be in the greatest light of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious:"
One whose protection is in the testimony that there is no god but Allah (swt) and that I am the Messenger of Allah (sw),
And who, when afflicted with a calamity, says:
Innā lillāhi wa innā ilayhi rāji‘ūn
"Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return"
And who, when blessed with goodness, says:
Al-ḥamdu lillāhi Rabbi al-‘ālamīn
"All praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds"
And one who, when committing a sin, says:
Astaghfiru Allāha wa atūbu ilayh
"I seek forgiveness from Allah (swt) and repent to Him."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.515 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Whenever a believer is afflicted with a calamity in this world and responds by reciting the phrase of 'istirja' (saying 'Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return') at the moment of calamity and exhibits patience, Allah (swt) forgives their past sins, except for major sins for which Allah (swt) has decreed Hellfire.
Additionally, each time the believer remembers that calamity in the future, recites 'istirja' again, and praises Allah (swt), Allah (swt) forgives all sins committed between the first instance of istirja' and the next, except for major sins."
records.arabicText: 515 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ يُصَابُ بِمُصِيبَةٍ فِي اَلدُّنْيَا فَيَسْتَرْجِعُ عِنْدَ مُصِيبَتِهِ وَ يَصْبِرُ حِينَ تَفْجَأُهُ اَلْمُصِيبَةُ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ مَا مَضَى مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ إِلاَّ اَلْكَبَائِرَ اَلَّتِي أَوْجَبَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَيْهَا اَلنَّارَ وَ كُلَّمَا ذَكَرَ مُصِيبَتَهُ فِيمَا يَسْتَقْبِلُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِ فَاسْتَرْجَعَ عِنْدَهَا وَ حَمِدَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عِنْدَهَا، غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ كُلَّ ذَنْبٍ اِكْتَسَبَهُ فِيمَا بَيْنَ اَلاِسْتِرْجَاعِ اَلْأَوَّلِ إِلَى اَلاِسْتِرْجَاعِ اَلْأَخِيرِ إِلاَّ اَلْكَبَائِرَ مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.515 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whenever a believer is afflicted with a calamity in this world and responds by reciting the phrase of 'istirja' (saying 'Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return') at the moment of calamity and exhibits patience, Allah (swt) forgives their past sins, except for major sins for which Allah (swt) has decreed Hellfire.
Additionally, each time the believer remembers that calamity in the future, recites 'istirja' again, and praises Allah (swt), Allah (swt) forgives all sins committed between the first instance of istirja' and the next, except for major sins."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.516 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
"There is an angel assigned to the cemeteries. When the family of the deceased returns home after the funeral, this angel takes a handful of soil and throws it after them, saying, 'Forget what you have seen.' Were it not for this, no one would find any benefit (joy) in living."
records.arabicText: 516 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِنَّ مَلَكاً مُوَكَّلاً بِالْمَقَابِرِ فَإِذَا اِنْصَرَفَ أَهْلُ اَلْمَيِّتِ مِنْ جَنَازَتِهِمْ عَنْ مَيِّتِهِمْ أَخَذَ قَبْضَةً مِنْ تُرَابٍ فَرَمَى بِهَا فِي آثَارِهِمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ اِنْسَوْا مَا رَأَيْتُمْ فَلَوْ لاَ ذَلِكَ مَا اِنْتَفَعَ أَحَدٌ بِعَيْشٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.516 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is an angel assigned to the cemeteries. When the family of the deceased returns home after the funeral, this angel takes a handful of soil and throws it after them, saying, 'Forget what you have seen.' Were it not for this, no one would find any benefit (joy) in living."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.517 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever is afflicted with a calamity, whether they express intense grief over it or not, whether they exercise patience or not, their reward from Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, is Paradise."
records.arabicText: 517 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ أُصِيبَ بِمُصِيبَةٍ جَزِعَ عَلَيْهَا أَوْ لَمْ يَجْزَعْ صَبَرَ عَلَيْهَا أَمْ لَمْ يَصْبِرْ كَانَ ثَوَابُهُ مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اَلْجَنَّةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.517 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever is afflicted with a calamity, whether they express intense grief over it or not, whether they exercise patience or not, their reward from Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, is Paradise."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.518 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The reward for a believer whose child dies is Paradise, whether they exhibit patience or not”.
records.arabicText: 518 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «ثَوَابُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ مِنْ وَلَدِهِ إِذَا مَاتَ اَلْجَنَّةُ صَبَرَ أَوْ لَمْ يَصْبِرْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.518 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The reward for a believer whose child dies is Paradise, whether they exhibit patience or not”.

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.519 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever loses a child (before their own passing), it is better for them than leaving behind seventy children after them, all of whom rode horses and fought in the way of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious."
records.arabicText: 519 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ قَدَّمَ وَلَداً كَانَ خَيْراً لَهُ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ يُخَلِّفُهُمْ بَعْدَهُ كُلُّهُمْ قَدْ رَكِبَ اَلْخَيْلَ وَ قَاتَلَ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.519 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever loses a child (before their own passing), it is better for them than leaving behind seventy children after them, all of whom rode horses and fought in the way of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.520 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"No one will enter Paradise unless they have a farat (one who goes ahead)."
A man who had no children and had not lost any asked:
"O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), does everyone among us have a farat?"
The Prophet replied: "Yes, among the farat of a believer is their brother in faith who preceded them (in death) for the sake of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious."
records.arabicText: 520 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «لاَ يَدْخُلُ اَلْجَنَّةَ رَجُلٌ لَيْسَ لَهُ فَرَطٌ» فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مِمَّنْ لَمْ يُولَدْ لَهُ وَ لَمْ يُقَدِّمْ وَلَداً يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ أَ وَ لِكُلِّنَا فَرَطٌ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ إِنَّ مِنْ فَرَطِ اَلرَّجُلِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ أَخَاهُ فِي اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.520 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"No one will enter Paradise unless they have a farat (one who goes ahead)."
A man who had no children and had not lost any asked:
"O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), does everyone among us have a farat?"
The Prophet replied: "Yes, among the farat of a believer is their brother in faith who preceded them (in death) for the sake of Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.521 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A} when Ja'far ibn Abi Talib was martyred:
"Do not supplicate with (expressions of) humiliation, loss, or desolation. Whatever you have said about him is true."
records.arabicText: 521 - وَقَالَ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِفَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ حِينَ قُتِلَ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ «لاَ تَدْعِي بِذُلٍّ وَ لاَ ثَكَلٍ وَ لاَ حَرَبٍ وَ مَا قُلْتِ فِيهِ فَقَدْ صَدَقْتِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.521 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A} when Ja'far ibn Abi Talib was martyred:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not supplicate with (expressions of) humiliation, loss, or desolation. Whatever you have said about him is true."

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.522 - it was narrated from Mehran ibn Muhammad that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When a person dies, Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, sends an angel to the one among their family who is most deeply affected by the loss. The angel touches their heart and alleviates the intensity of grief. If it were not for this, the world would not be able to continue”.
records.arabicText: 522 - وَ رَوَى مِهْرَانُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِنَّ اَلْمَيِّتَ إِذَا مَاتَ بَعَثَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَلَكاً إِلَى أَوْجَعِ أَهْلِهِ عَلَيْهِ فَمَسَحَ عَلَى قَلْبِهِ فَأَنْسَاهُ لَوْعَةَ اَلْحُزْنِ لَوْ لاَ ذَلِكَ لَمْ تُعْمَرِ اَلدُّنْيَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.522 - it was narrated from Mehran ibn Muhammad that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a person dies, Allah (swt), the Exalted and Glorious, sends an angel to the one among their family who is most deeply affected by the loss. The angel touches their heart and alleviates the intensity of grief. If it were not for this, the world would not be able to continue”.

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.523 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"When the child of a believer passes away, Allah (swt) – knowing best what His servant has said – asks the angels: 'Have you taken the child of My believing servant?'
They reply: 'Yes, our Lord (azj).'
Allah (swt) then asks: 'What did My servant say?'
They say: "He praised You and said: "Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return"
Then Allah (swt), the Exalted, says: "'Build for My servant a house in Paradise and name it the House of Praise (Bayt al-Hamd).'"
records.arabicText: 523 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «إِذَا قُبِضَ وَلَدُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ وَ اَللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا قَالَ اَلْعَبْدُ فَيَسْأَلُ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةَ قَبَضْتُمْ وَلَدَ فُلاَنٍ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ فَيَقُولُونَ نَعَمْ رَبَّنَا فَيَقُولُ فَمَا ذَا قَالَ عَبْدِيَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ فَيَقُولُونَ حَمِدَكَ رَبَّنَا وَ اِسْتَرْجَعَ فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ اِبْنُوا لَهُ بَيْتاً فِي اَلْجَنَّةِ ' وَ سَمُّوهُ بَيْتَ اَلْحَمْدِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.523 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the child of a believer passes away, Allah (swt) – knowing best what His servant has said – asks the angels: 'Have you taken the child of My believing servant?'
They reply: 'Yes, our Lord (azj).'
Allah (swt) then asks: 'What did My servant say?'
They say: "He praised You and said: "Indeed, we belong to Allah (swt) and to Him we will return"
Then Allah (swt), the Exalted, says: "'Build for My servant a house in Paradise and name it the House of Praise (Bayt al-Hamd).'"

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.524 - When Ismail ibn Jaafar passed away, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) came out and walked ahead of the bier without shoes and without an outer garment (ridā').
records.arabicText: 524 - وَ لَمَّا مَاتَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ خَرَجَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَتَقَدَّمَ اَلسَّرِيرَ بِلاَ حِذَاءٍ وَ لاَ رِدَاءٍ.

records.chapter: The Chapter of Condolences and Grief During Calamities, Visiting Graves, Lamentation and Mourning Gatherings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.525 - When Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as), saw a funeral procession, He would say:
Alhamdulillahi alladhi lam yaj‘alni mina as-sawadi al-mukhtaram
'Praise be to Allah (swt) who did not make me from among those whose lives were abruptly taken'".
records.arabicText: 525 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا رَأَى جَنَازَةً قَالَ « اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ اَلَّذِي لَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي مِنَ اَلسَّوَادِ اَلْمُخْتَرَمِ.
